quote_of_day.text:
I think we are the worst race in the world

We nor have image of nigger or living standards of whites. And even war torn shitholes like Palestine looks cleaner than india. South asians including Pakis and kanglus are worst species along with us. The most subhuman race in the world along with us
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste tension moved over the board today, bringing scattered showers of argument. Anon's remark about 'post hand' sparked a debate that drew a significant following.

A cold front of exclusionary rhetoric blew in later, with one anon proclaiming the board as a space for 'North Indian Aryans only' and denouncing Dravidian individuals. This brought a brief but intense downpour of protests and counter-accusations.

Meanwhile, a high-pressure ridge of self-loathing formed over a section of the board, where an anon claimed that South Asians are the 'worst species' and bemoaned the region's living standards.

Nearby, a speculative fog rolled in around a chatbot's supposed caste origins, with one user noting its unexpected reference to a 'brahmin bull.'

articles.title: Anon sparks debate with 'Post hand' remark
articles.body:
Anon opened the discussion with a simple post saying 'Post hand'. One anon responded with 'Post thighs to prove you are Aryan', suggesting a connection between the original post and claims of Aryan identity.

Another anon chimed in with 'yaar uncjeet', seemingly referencing the original post. The conversation quickly devolved into a series of seemingly unrelated and joking remarks.

Anon claimed to be '18' with 'very fast metabolism' and 'mid tall' explaining their appearance. Another anon sarcastically referred to this as 'literal scooty mechanics on this forum'.

The topic of Aryan identity and 'kanging' was brought up, with one anon saying 'Remind me again who is doing that aryan kanging again?' and another agreeing 'Kek that anon was right about Aryan kangers'.

The thread also included several requests for users to post images of their hands, with one anon saying 'post your hands now'.

Throughout the discussion, users exchanged brief and often nonsensical remarks, including 'yaar nanhe jeet', 'God got atleast u post spoilers', and 'My thigs'. One anon simply posted 'My hand' in response to a request.

The tone of the discussion was largely joking and sarcastic, with users seemingly trying to provoke reactions from one another.

articles.title: Caste and Varna in Vedic Times
articles.body:
Anon opened a discussion on the distinction between caste (jati) and varna in Vedic times, stating that while caste was fixed, varna was theoretical and changeable, with inter-varna marriages supposedly allowed among Aryas.

Another anon, Himachali gunda, responded sceptically to this theory, arguing that Varna was scriptural and theoretical, similar to Confucian 'four occupations', while the lived reality on the ground was a jati system, not a varna system.

A respondent expressed eugenicist views, saying they preferred it over casteism, and made violent remarks about certain castes. Anon also brought up the example of Ahirandi adopting the surname 'Yadav' in the 1920 Ahirandi Mahasabha.

One anon referred to someone as 'chamar', using it as an insult, and another mentioned 'Lord Varuna' in response to a query about the 'king of asuras'.

A different anon jokingly or seriously mentioned 'yaar padav', which seemed to be a non-sequitur. The discussion reflects various perspectives on caste and varna, with some posters questioning traditional theories and others expressing extreme views.

articles.title: Caste-based bans spark debate on Indiachan
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion by stating that Indiachan is an 'Aryan board' and that 'Dravidian abbo subhumans' are not allowed. This assertion immediately drew responses from other users.

One anon expressed concern that such exclusions could lead to mod abuse, where users are banned based on personal reasons rather than community guidelines.

This user questioned the point of an Indian board if it resorts to similar practices, suggesting that a 'plebbit' (likely a reference to a more open or less moderated platform) would be preferable.

Another anon reacted strongly against the idea of range-banning users from certain states or caste groups, threatening to leave the board if such actions are taken. This user emphasized having an open mind, distinct from their linguistic or regional background.

The conversation also took a darker turn with personal attacks and casteist slurs being thrown around. One user sarcastically suggested that they might convert to Islam if they are not accepted on the board. Others responded with hostility, using derogatory terms.

Some users jokingly suggested renaming the board to 'Aryachan' if it continues to enforce Aryan-only policies. One anon also questioned the admin's stance on these developments.

The thread showcases a mix of reactions, from concerns about moderation practices to aggressive personal attacks and casteist remarks.

articles.title: Anon claims South Asians are 'worst species'
articles.body: Anon posted that Indians are the 'worst race in the world', lacking the image of a desirable group or living standards of whites. They claimed even war-torn areas like Palestine appear cleaner than India. One respondent agreed, suggesting Indians should learn cleanliness from 'niggers'. Another anon boasted about hypothetical bullying of Indians if they were born in the first world. Some anons engaged in counter-posting and name-calling.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on Chatbot's Caste Origins
articles.body: Anon mentioned a chatbot generated an idea about a 'brahmin bull' without being prompted. Another anon responded, calling the idea 'corny nigga' and joking about a 'honda' creating it. One anon suggested the chatbot's creator might be a Dravidian, prompting another to sarcastically comment, 'Saar he’s Dravidian Saar! Post hand.' The conversation included various comments about caste and Aryan identity, with one anon stating, 'Aryan larpers got outed.'
